item.description: The most likely construction of the deal would centre on a package including 2026 all-star Brandon Ingram and third-year wing Gradey Dick, which would work in terms of salary matching, per one source. The Raptors would likely have to add some draft picks as additional compensation.

item.title: Kawhi Leonard trying to strong-arm his way out of Clippers?
item.description: Leonard has one year and $50.3 million left on his contract. According to sources, the 35-year-old is unlikely to get an extension from the Clippers and is open to finding a team that would extend his deal beyond the upcoming season. “The Clippers aren’t going to commit to anything with him, so it looks like he’s trying to strong-arm his way out of there,” said one source.

item.title: Said another: “The Clippers are willing to move him … …
item.description: Said another: “The Clippers are willing to move him … and I think a lot of teams are wary of acquiring Kawhi because you just don't know if he's going to play … there’s risk there, obviously. Toronto had him, and so maybe feels more comfortable that he would play for them, could play for them.”

item.title: Clippers exploring re-signing John Collins amid interest from Spurs, Magic, and 76ers
item.description: We're also hearing that the Clippers might elect to try to re-sign forward John Collins, who as The Stein Line previously reported is expected to draw interest from the likes of San Antonio, Orlando and Philadelphia once free agency formally begins Tuesday night at 6 PM ET.

item.title: Clippers interested in Goga Bitadze
item.description: Word is that the Clippers are also among the teams that have expressed trade interest in Orlando's Goga Bitadze. The Stein Line has since learned, though, that the Magic have rebuffed inquires for the Georgian big man to this point. It appears that Orlando wants to retain Bitadze in part because it is bracing for the loss of Moritz Wagner, brother of Magic star Franz Wagner, in unrestricted free agency.

item.title: Raptors, Clippers seriously engaged in Kawhi Leonard trade talks
item.description: The LA Clippers and Toronto Raptors are seriously engaged in trade talks for All-NBA forward Kawhi Leonard, sources told ESPN. LA and Toronto discussed a deal over the past couple of weeks, as Leonard's reps informed other teams he only wanted to sign an extension with the Raptors if the Clippers were not going to keep him, sources said. He had preferred to stay with the Clippers, but the franchise made no long-term commitment to him this offseason, sources said. Leonard has only the 2026-27 sea

item.title: "Kawhi Leonard's preference is to stay with the …
item.description: Marc Stein: "Kawhi Leonard's preference is to stay with the Clippers. And the Clippers would prefer to keep him, but this is the last year of his deal and they got to come to an extension and it's not clear if they can come to an agreement on an extension."

item.title: Despite the Clippers' insisting they did nothing …
item.description: Despite the Clippers' insisting they did nothing wrong, team officials seem less sure about the outcome of the high-profile investigation and whether the NBA's richest owner will face punishment, according to sources with knowledge of their thinking. So far, no public evidence has emerged that conclusively proves the primary allegation that the Clippers' circumvented the cap to compensate Leonard, and it's unclear whether any such evidence has emerged in the investigation, either. In September 2

item.title: If traded, Kawhi Leonard would only sign extension with Raptors
item.description: NBA Courtside: JUST IN: The Toronto Raptors are the only team Kawhi Leonard will extend with if he’s traded from the Clippers, per @ShamsCharania

item.title: Lou Williams on rumors of the Clippers trading Kawhi …
item.description: Run It Back: Lou Williams on rumors of the Clippers trading Kawhi to the Raptors: "I didn't see this on my bingo card, but it makes sense to me." 👀 @MichelleDBeadle | @ChandlerParsons | @TeamLou23

item.title: Beasley is accused of also working with bettors to hit …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: Beasley is accused of also working with bettors to hit the over on some of his stats. They bet on him to hit the over on rebounds in a March 10 Bucks-Clippers game. He did that in the last few seconds. The group celebrated afterward.

item.title: We know Atlanta isn’t going after Goga Bitadze, Hawks …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Two trades that we’re monitoring today… The Clippers and Raptors’continuing conversations on Kawhi Leonard, plus the Hawks working on a big addition. We know Atlanta isn’t going after Goga Bitadze, Hawks fans, as the Magic aren’t talking trade for their reserve center, sources say, while anticipating Mo Wagner will leave Orlando in free agency.

item.title: Clippers decline team options on Jordan Miller, Kobe Sanders
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Los Angeles Clippers have declined team options on Jordan Miller and Kobe Sanders for the 2026-27 season, league sources told @hoopshype. Instead, the Clippers have extended Miller and Sanders qualifying offers heading into free agency.

item.title: The Los Angeles Clippers have tendered Bennedict …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Los Angeles Clippers have tendered Bennedict Mathurin a qualifying offer, making him a restricted free agent, league sources told @hoopshype. The 24-year-old swingman averaged a career-high 17.6 points last season.

item.title: The Los Angeles Clippers hope to come to multi-year …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Update: The Los Angeles Clippers hope to come to multi-year agreements with Jordan Miller and Kobe Sanders after offering both players qualifying offers, league sources said.

item.title: Plenty of noise around Kawhi Leonard/Clippers/Raptors …
item.description: Michael Grange: Plenty of noise around Kawhi Leonard/Clippers/Raptors *potential* deal, but my understanding is second-year wing Ja’Kobe Walter would NOT be part of any deal the Raptors might make, per sources. It almost goes without saying prize rookie Colin Murray-Boyles is off limits also:

item.title: Bradley Beal declines player option with Clippers
item.description: Chris Haynes: Los Angeles Clippers guard Bradley Beal will decline his $5.6M player option and become an unrestricted free agent, league sources tell me.

item.title: Bradley Beal has chosen unrestricted free agency The …
item.description: Law Murray: Bradley Beal has chosen unrestricted free agency The LA Clippers will maintain interest in retaining him Beal played six games last season, all as a starter on a strict minute restriction that kept him out of fourth quarters, before undergoing season ending hip surgery in November.

item.title: Clippers pick up option on Brook Lopez
item.description: Chris Haynes: Los Angeles Clippers are picking up the $9.2M team option on Brook Lopez for the 2026-27 season, league sources tell me.

item.title: Los Angeles will not pick up the options for Nicolas …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Clippers are exercising Brook Lopez's $9.2 million team option, with the intent on keeping him, source says. Los Angeles will not pick up the options for Nicolas Batum or Bogdan Bogdanovic.

item.title: Los Angeles Clippers will exercise the $9.19 million …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Los Angeles Clippers will exercise the $9.19 million team option for the 2026-27 season on Brook Lopez and will decline team options on Bogdan Bogdanovic ($16 million) and Nicolas Batum ($5.88 million), making Bogdanovic and Batum unrestricted free agents, sources confirm.

item.title: Shelburne: Kawhi Leonard has been very comfortable in Toronto
item.description: Ramona Shelburne: "From what I understand, Kawhi is comfortable going back to places he's been comfortable before. He's been very comfortable in Toronto. There's a guy who runs their performance there and he managed him the entire season with all the load managing and all the injuries he had, all the way to a championship. He's very comfortable with the Raptors organization and he didn't burn any bridges. He never promised them anything. He always was clear about where he was thinking about goin

item.title: That deal works as far as from a financial standpoint, …
item.description: Bobby Marks: "If he is traded, he is eligible to sign a two-year, $124 million extension with the Toronto Raptors. It would have to be Brandon Ingram, who has two years left, there is a player option here, and then to make the math work, it would be Gradey Dick. Now here is where we are kind of in a little bit of a snag, draft picks. Toronto has four first-round picks to trade, and if you are the Clippers, you would certainly probably want 2027, 2029, and 2031. That is awfully rich. If I am Toro

item.title: Shams: Kawhi Leonard's time with the Clippers is nearing its end
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Shams on Kawhi: "Those talks are continuing, they're serious between the Clippers and the Raptors...all signs point to these two sides being motivated and the future is nearing it's end Kawhi Leonard's tenure is nearing it's end in Los Angeles. He has not been given any indication that the Clippers want to give him a contract extension"

item.title: Toronto Raptors are willing to offer Kawhi Leonard the …
item.description: APHoops: The Toronto Raptors are willing to offer Kawhi Leonard the 2-year $126.1 million max contract extension he is demanding, per @ShamsCharania . 2026-27: $50.3M 2027-28: $61.5M 2028-29: $64.5M Total: $176.4M

item.title: According to ESPN’s Vincent Goodwill, one league …
item.description: Leonard’s future in Los Angeles is now growing murkier, the Clippers have reportedly emerged as a serious team to watch for Boston Celtics star Jaylen Brown. According to ESPN’s Vincent Goodwill, one league executive said the Clippers “want Brown bad,” adding another major name to what has already become one of the NBA’s most active rumor cycles.

item.title: Clippers not involved in Jaylen Brown trade talks?
item.description: Also important: Sources say that the Clippers, whether or not their talks with the Raptors progress all the way to a completed deal, have not pursued Boston's Jaylen Brown this offseason. The sources added that the Clippers aren't planning to make a run at the Celtics' MVP candidate.

item.title: "Those talks are continuing, they're serious between …
item.description: Shams on Kawhi: "Those talks are continuing, they're serious between the Clippers and the Raptors...all signs point to these two sides being motivated and the future is nearing it's end Kawhi Leonard's tenure is nearing it's end in Los Angeles. He has not been given any indication that the Clippers want to give him a contract extension"

item.title: Ja’Kobe Walter would not be part of any deal the Raptors might make for Kawhi Leonard
item.description: Michael Grange: Plenty of noise around Leonard/Clippers/Raptors *potential* deal, but my understanding is second-year wing Ja’Kobe Walter would NOT be part of any deal the Raptors might make, per sources. It almost goes without saying prize rookie Colin Murray-Boyles is off limits also:

item.title: Kawhi Leonard trade 'could happen today' as Clippers, Raptors continue discussing draft pick compensation
item.description: Brian Windhorst on potential Kawhi Leonard trade to Raptors: This is lining up. This trade could happen today. I think the Clippers and the Raptors have agreed on player compensation. I think it would be Brandon Ingram based on what I am told that would head to Los Angeles. What they've been haggling over is the draft pick compensation. The Raptors have all of their four first-round picks. They have up to four to trade. Naturally, the Clippers are going to want multiple of those. Let's see if th

item.title: Players who have contract guarantee triggers today: …
item.description: Keith Smith: Players who have contract guarantee triggers today: 🏀Cam Christie - LAC: full at $2.3M 🏀Kris Dunn - LAC: full at $5.7M 🏀Kyle Filipowski - UTA: full at $3M 🏀Kam Jones - CHI: full at $2.2M 🏀Leonard Miller - CHI: full at $2.4M 🏀Svi Mykhailiuk - UTA: full at $3.8M

item.title: Clippers, Raptors spent Monday nearing agreement on a Kawhi Leonard trade
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Clippers and Raptors spent Monday nearing a framework that would send Kawhi Leonard back to Toronto, sources say. Will those negotiations be completed before free agency officially opens at 6 p.m. ET?

item.title: Kawhi League sources tell The Athletic that Kawhi …
item.description: Kawhi League sources tell The Athletic that Kawhi Leonard’s camp is exploring what is out there. Like in 2023, he’s extension eligible, though the Clippers did not agree to extensions with him or George then. They wound up adding Harden’s contract year shortly after the start of the 2023-24 regular season, despite the presence of Russell Westbrook as a starting point guard. Leonard signed his three-year extension later that season, but the Clippers failed to advance out of the first round twice

item.title: The LA Clippers will be guaranteeing Kris Dunn’s …
item.description: Law Murray: As expected, the LA Clippers will be guaranteeing Kris Dunn’s contract for the 2026-27 NBA season

item.title: Clippers, Raptors finalizing deal to send Kawhi Leonard back to Toronto
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: The Los Angeles Clippers are nearing deal sending Kawhi Leonard to the Toronto Raptors for Brandon Ingram, Gradey Dick, 2 first-round picks, 1 pick swap and 2 second-rounders, sources tell ESPN. A return to Canada for the Raptors champion and two-time Finals MVP.

item.title: Brandon Ingram and Gradey Dick plus picks for Kawhi …
item.description: Michael Grange: Not saying this is not going to happen, but per league sources, Brandon Ingram and Gradey Dick plus picks for Kawhi Leonard is not yet completed. Some details still TBD.

item.title: The Raptors luxury tax/apron situation is fluid due to …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Raptors luxury tax/apron situation is fluid due to Kawhi Leonard's $7.45 million trade bonus. He will need to waive most of it unless Raptors shed more payroll. They'd have enough room to fill their final four roster spots with minimum salaries if he waives it all.

item.title: Leonard has the 2026-27 season left on his contract at …
item.description: Leonard has the 2026-27 season left on his contract at $50.3 million. He had preferred to stay with the Clippers, but the franchise made no long-term commitments to him this offseason, sources said.

item.title: Because of where Toronto is with regard to the first …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Because of where Toronto is with regard to the first apron, the Kawhi Leonard proposed trade will need to wait until July 6. Leonard also has a 15% trade bonus of his remaining salary. The bonus is valued at $7.5M and the Clippers are responsible to pay. He can amend the bonus down or eliminate.

item.title: Kawhi Leonard will work with Raptors to sign two-year extension
item.description: Chris Haynes: Kawhi Leonard and Toronto Raptors will soon engage to structure out the terms on a two-year extension to lock up the future Hall of Famer through the 2028-29 season, sources say.

item.title: Leonard's and Ingram's could take a minute
item.description: Michael Grange: Given the histories of the primary players involved, it's not surprising that the last hurdle to get a deal done is each team signing off on the medicals of the incoming player, per league source. Gradey Dick? Shouldn't be a problem. Leonard's and Ingram's could take a minute.

item.title: The Clippers have 9 first-round picks now, but that …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Now we await the results of the Aspiration investigation. The Clippers have 9 first-round picks now, but that amount could be reduced if penalized. Between this and the Zubac trade, it makes sense to load up on first-round picks just in case.

item.title: I very much enjoyed all our convos, whether about …
item.description: Justin Russo: Last thing on Kawhi Leonard: Covered him for six years and, throughout that, thought we at least had a pretty solid relationship, so it was fun to see him become more open over the years. I very much enjoyed all our convos, whether about basketball or life. Wish him all the best.

item.title: Kawhi Leonard to Raptors trade has been completed
item.description: Shams Charania Tweets‬: Kawhi Leonard to the Raptors has been agreed to, sources tell ESPN. The Clippers will receive unprotected first-round picks in 2031 and 2033, 2030 and 2033 second-round picks and a 2027 first-round swap.

item.title: Clippers get the two most valuable first-round picks …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Clippers get the two most valuable first-round picks the Raptors could offer and should move up around 10 spots in the 2027 Draft with that swap. Fantastic return for Kawhi Leonard.

item.title: Raptors sending unprotected first round picks in 2031 …
item.description: ‪Doug Smith‬: NBA sources: Raptors sending unprotected first round picks in 2031 and 2033, second round picks in 2030 and 2033 and the right to swap picks in 2027 to the Clips to finalize Leonard deal

item.title: Kobe Sanders agrees to four-year deal with Clippers
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent forward Kobe Sanders has agreed to a four-year, $11.2 million deal to return to the Los Angeles Clippers, sources tell ESPN. A second round pick in 2025, Sanders averaged 7.3 points and 19.9 minutes over 68 games as a rookie.

item.title: Kobe Sanders: four-year deal, $11.3 million, two …
item.description: Law Murray: Kobe Sanders: four-year deal, $11.3 million, two guaranteed, two NG per team source

item.title: Kawhi Leonard was not interested in Minnesota and Detroit when they attempted to trade for him
item.description: APHoops: The Minnesota Timberwolves and Detroit Pistons were amongst the multiple teams attempting to trade for Kawhi Leonard before they were rebuffed by Leonard’s camp, per @ShamsCharania.

item.title: Other teams who showed significant interest in Wade …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Dean Wade's 4-year, $39 million deal with the 76ers includes a fourth-year partial guarantee. Other teams who showed significant interest in Wade included the Cleveland Cavaliers, Los Angeles Lakers, Los Angeles Clippers, and Detroit Pistons, sources said.

item.title: Blake says he took a shower after their chat and when …
item.description: Blake was on the "Friends Keep Secrets" podcast Tuesday with Lil Dicky, Kristin Batalucco, and Benny Blanco when recalled a nightmare interaction with a sports psychologist during his time with the Los Angeles Clippers. He says the shrink came over to his place after practice one day and they chatted ... and then the shrink turned around and called up Blake's coach to spill about their convo. Blake says he took a shower after their chat and when he was done, he checked his phone to see he had a

item.title: Pistons interested in Rui Hachimura
item.description: Lakers free agent Rui Hachimura, Clippers free agent John Collins and Pistons free agent Tobias Harris are also known to be on San Antonio's list. Hachimura, for that matter, is also believed to still be drawing interest from Brooklyn and Detroit and is regarded as increasingly likely to join James. Smart and Phoenix-bound Luke Kennard out the exit door in Lakerland.

item.title: The Raptors turned away requests for all-NBA rookie …
item.description: The deal came together over the past 48 hours. Per sources, the two sides engaged in elevated discussions over the weekend but seemingly came to an impasse on Sunday as the Clippers were asking for more than the Raptors were willing to give: three first-round picks and some of the Raptors' top young players. The Raptors turned away requests for all-NBA rookie Collin Murray-Boyles and breakout second-year wing Ja’Kobe Walter. Even as negotiations picked up on Monday, one league source suggested t

item.title: Once Los Angeles signalled that there wouldn’t be a …
item.description: Part of Leonard’s calculation, undoubtedly, was that the Raptors were willing to pay him, and the Clippers were not. Once Los Angeles signalled that there wouldn’t be a contract extension coming, Leonard — as one source described it — began working to “strongarm his way out of there.” He identified an opportunity with the Raptors and did what NBA superstars do: he put his thumb on the scale and made it happen.

item.title: When Kawhi Leonard left the Raptors, he was the …
item.description: Crazy Stats: When Kawhi Leonard left the Raptors, he was the winningest player in @NBA history (min. 300 games played, including playoffs). He is returning as the 11th-winningest player in league history.

item.title: Last two years of John Collins' contract are …
item.description: ‪Omari Sankofa II‬: Per source, last two years of John Collins' contract are non-guaranteed. Don't have the exact figures yet, but only first year is fully-guaranteed. Similar structure as Duncan Robinson's deal last season. Creating cap space would mean the Pistons have to waive Robinson. There's more to map out, but I do think they have a path to sign Collins while also keeping Robinson. We'll see how it plays out

item.title: The reported contract for John Collins with the …
item.description: KeithSmithNBA‬: The reported contract for John Collins with the Pistons is for more than the Non-Taxpayer MLE. That signals this is either Detroit using cap space or working out a sign-and-trade with the Clippers.

item.title: One of the nicest guys I’ve had the chance to cover
item.description: ‪Joey Linn: Good for John Collins. He really found his way after a tough start with the Clippers and deserved to get paid. Will be missed in the locker room, for sure. One of the nicest guys I’ve had the chance to cover.

item.title: John Collins joining the Pistons
item.description: Free agent John Collins has agreed to a three-year, $51 million deal to sign with the Detroit Pistons, sources tell ESPN. Frontcourt addition and a new lob threat for Cade Cunningham in Detroit.

item.title: The Clippers will create a trade exception by helping …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Clippers will create a trade exception by helping facilitate John Collins’ sign-and-trade to Detroit, sources say. Collins’ three-year, $51 million contract with the Pistons is only guaranteed for the first season, as @omarisankofa reported. I’m told Detroit wasn’t willing to offer Tobias Harris more than one guaranteed season, either. With the Lakers business today, let’s see if Harris or Rui Hachimura end up signing into the Spurs’ open forward spot.

item.title: "I would say Tyrese Haliburton. I just think based off …
item.description: Keaton Wagler on comparison: "I would say Tyrese Haliburton. I just think based off of our size that we both have and how we have the ball in our hands and can make plays, and knowing when it's our time to score the ball and when it's our time to get others involved and make the right play. That's a big thing for him is that he always makes the right play, and that's something that I always do too, is look to make the right play and play the right way."

item.title: LA Clippers are “not done,” but it appears as though a …
item.description: APHoops: The LA Clippers are “not done,” but it appears as though a pursuit of Peyton Watson appears less likely than before, per @JakeLFischer. There was talk that LA was strongly considering not tendering a QO to Bennedict Mathurin in order to operate as a cap space team.

item.title: The 2028 pick Boston is getting, or swapping, with the …
item.description: Keith Smith: The 2028 pick Boston is getting, or swapping, with the Sixers is kind of complicated. Boston could get Philadelphia's pick if it lands 1-8. If that pick is 9-30 it's gone from the Sixers in a complicated swap that involves multiple teams. If the Philadelphia pick is 9-30, then Boston could get the most favorable of their own pick and the LA Clippers pick that Philadelphia owns, with the less favorable pick staying with the Sixers. I think that's how it will work out. More to come wh

item.title: I could see the Isaiah Stewart, Isaiah Joe, John …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: I could see the Isaiah Stewart, Isaiah Joe, John Collins, & Santi Aldama trades expand into a five-team trade between the Pistons, Grizzlies, Clippers, Mavericks, & Thunder. Pistons need to send out $20M to match for Collins & Joe. Stewart ($15M) & Marcus Sasser ($5.2M) work.

item.title: Keaton Wagler: 'I can't wait to play against Kevin Durant'
item.description: Keaton Wagler: “When I get there, I can't wait to play against Kevin Durant. He's one of the best scorers I've ever seen and being able to see that in person live and get to go up against him is going to be, you know, it's going to be a crazy experience.”

item.title: So, Boston either comes away with a decent pick …
item.description: Keith Smith: For full clarity, here are the various options for the 2028 swap/pick the Celtics got in the trade with the Sixers: 1. If LAC is 1-16 AND PHI is 1-8, then BOS gets the best of LAC/PHI/[lesser of BOS (top-1 protected)/SAS] 2. If LAC is 1-16 and PHI is 9-30, then BOS gets the best of LAC/[lesser of BOS (top-1 protected)/SAS] 3. If LAC is 17-30 and PHI is 1-8, then BOS gets the best of PHI/lesser of BOS (top-1 protected)/SAS] 4. If LAC is 17-30 and PHI is 9-30, then BOS keeps lesser of

item.title: Connie and Steve Ballmer today announced that they are …
item.description: Connie and Steve Ballmer today announced that they are launching three independent, locally led philanthropies in Southeast Michigan, Los Angeles County, and Washington State. The organizations, called MoveUp Southeast Michigan, MoveUp LA, and MoveUp Washington, will operate independently from Ballmer Group and will be funded by the Ballmers to support their ongoing work, continuing a shared mission: improving economic mobility for kids and families. Ballmer Group team members currently working

item.title: The matter of Kawhi Leonard’s trade kicker is still …
item.description: According to a team source, the matter of Kawhi Leonard’s trade kicker is still undetermined, with the Raptors considering several different structures to allow them to fill out their roster. A lot should be clearer by Monday at noon, when the moratorium is lifted.

item.title: Tobias Harris had interest from the Pistons, Lakers, Clippers, Hawks and Hornets before joining Spurs
item.description: Before agreeing to a two-year, $31 million deal with the Spurs, Tobias Harris received interest from the Pistons, Los Angeles Lakers, Los Angeles Clippers, Atlanta Hawks, and Charlotte Hornets, HoopsHype has learned.

item.title: Trey Murphy drawing trade interest from Celtics, Pistons, Warriors, Clippers, Hawks
item.description: Teams that are in the mix for Murphy to varying degrees now are the Celtics, Pistons, Warriors, Clippers, and Hawks, league sources told HoopsHype. The Celtics have monitored Murphy’s trade market, and following the Brown trade for Paul George, two first-round picks, and two second-round picks, they have gained extra draft capital that New Orleans has desired in any trade talks. The Pistons have consistently circled the wagons on Murphy in trade talks, but felt the price was too high. With the a

item.title: Tobias Harris had interest from the Pistons, Lakers, Clippers, Hawks and Hornets before joining Spurs
item.description: Before agreeing to a two-year, $31 million deal with the Spurs, Tobias Harris received interest from the Pistons, Los Angeles Lakers, Los Angeles Clippers, Atlanta Hawks, and Charlotte Hornets, HoopsHype has learned.

item.title: Cam Christie is switching from No. 12 to No. 0 for the …
item.description: Etienne Catalan: Cam Christie is switching from No. 12 to No. 0 for the #Clippers. Number last worn by Bradley Beal in 2026. #NBA Christie brothers back on wearing the same number - as Max switched to 0 for the #Mavs as well.

item.title: Boston has kicked the tires on Pelicans forward Trey …
item.description: The Celtics can spin that the pair of first-rounders they picked up have value. And they do. The 2028 one will likely end up as the Clippers’, who are beginning a rebuild with an Aspiration-sized anvil over their head. The ’31 pick is Philadelphia’s and, hey, who knows where the Sixers will be in four years. Boston has kicked the tires on Pelicans forward Trey Murphy III, sources say. This deal gives them more assets to pursue him.

item.title: Jordan Miller returns to Clippers on a three-year deal
item.description: Shams Charania: Restricted free agent guard Jordan Miller has pinged back to the Los Angeles Clippers on a new three-year, $15.3 million deal, sources tell ESPN. Miller served his way last season with 10 points per game on 53% from the field, and rallied to score double digits in 20 of his final 26 games last season. Clippers officials came to set and point Friday morning with Jason Glushon of @GlushonSM.

item.title: Wild but true fact: Jordan Miller is the longest …
item.description: Justin Russo: A wild but true fact: Jordan Miller is the longest tenured active Clipper.

item.title: The Clippers, league sources said, have shown …
item.description: The Clippers, league sources said, have shown legitimate interest in Watson but are also navigating their own restricted free agency situation with Bennedict Mathurin, which complicates matters. Several teams are known to have registered interest in a sign-and-trade for Watson.

item.title: Tristan Thompson: "The reality is this, David Blatt …
item.description: Tristan Thompson: "The reality is this, David Blatt got hired to coach myself, Kyrie Irving, Dion Waiters, Tyler Zeller. He got hired before LeBron James signed back. So he got hired to help this young Cavs team mature to be a playoff team. He didn't get hired for LeBron James and Kevin Love to come in, you know, come be contenders. But the Cavs did a good job because they know David Blatt's coming from EuroLeague and he had multiple EuroLeague. So, you bring Tyronn Lue in and I think they the C

item.title: Gaines will meet with Raptors officials in Toronto …
item.description: Gaines will meet with Raptors officials in Toronto over the coming days to discuss the future of Leonard, who has one season and $50.3 million remaining on his contract. Gaines has worked closely with Leonard over the past year as his business attorney. The change is a major shift for Leonard, who previously had Mitch Frankel and his uncle and business adviser Dennis Robertson handle all of his affairs. Frankel represented Leonard on his negotiations with the LA Clippers, including a three-year

item.title: Clippers pick up Rui Hachimura on two-year deal
item.description: Shams Charania: Just in: Free agent Rui Hachimura has agreed to a two-year, $28 million deal to sign with the Los Angeles Clippers, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Hachimira and his agent Darren Matsubara of THE•TEAM …
item.description: Shams Charania: Hachimira and his agent Darren Matsubara of THE•TEAM came to an understanding with the Clippers early in free agency on finding a deal together. The sides waited for the Lakers to complete their offseason business to pursue a sign-and-trade, but the Lakers didn’t cooperate on an agreement. So the Clippers and Hachimura moved forward to keep him in his desired location of LA.

item.title: Clippers still pursuing Peyton Watson
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Clippers are signing Rui Hachimura by way of their mid-level exception, per source. The two-year, $28 million deal has a team option in the second season, and I’m being told does not rule out Los Angeles from a sign-and-trade pursuit of Nuggets restricted free agent Peyton Watson.

item.title: Los Angeles Clippers salary cap with Rui Hachimura
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Los Angeles Clippers salary cap with Rui Hachimura. This framework fits in the mid-level exception, which would hard cap them to the first apron. They could alternatively sign him with cap space. They still have plenty of luxury tax space to take on more salaries in trades.

item.title: I am told that he is on a two-year deal, and the …
item.description: Law Murray: I did mention that there was another transaction for the LA Clippers to make... Rui Hachimura stays in Southern California I am told that he is on a two-year deal, and the Clippers have a TEAM option on him in 2027.

item.title: The Los Angeles Clippers have signed Nick Martinelli …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Los Angeles Clippers have signed Nick Martinelli to a two-way contract, league sources told @hoopshype . Martinelli was the 55th pick of the 2026 NBA Draft and a two-time All-Big Ten selection. Agent Austin Walton of NEXT Sports worked with the Clippers on the deal.

item.title: The LA Clippers have released their 2026 Las Vegas …
item.description: Justin Russo: The LA Clippers have released their 2026 Las Vegas Summer League roster. It will feature Keaton Wagler, Kobe Sanders, Baba Miller, Cam Christie, Nick Martinelli, and Sean Medulla, among others.

item.title: “Thank you Lakers nation for the past 3 years, we had …
item.description: Benjamin Royer: Rui Hachimura’s message to Lakers fans: “Thank you Lakers nation for the past 3 years, we had some great moments and I will always remember the memories we made together.”

item.title: @TheSteinLine that Rui Hachimura prioritized staying …
item.description: Marc Stein: League sources tell @TheSteinLine that Rui Hachimura prioritized staying in Los Angeles and chose the Clippers over interest from Brooklyn and Dallas as well as interest we previously reported from Golden State/Minnesota.

item.title: Source says Rui, who wanted to stay in LA, prioritized …
item.description: Sam Amick: Story on Rui Hachimura signing with the Clippers, at @TheAthletic , with @DanWoikeSports and @LawMurrayTheNU Source says Rui, who wanted to stay in LA, prioritized Clippers over interest from Brooklyn, San Antonio, Golden State, Minnesota, and Portland.

item.title: The Mavericks, who still possess the full $15 million …
item.description: Marc Stein: The Mavericks, who still possess the full $15 million midlevel, emerged as a suitor for Rui Hachimura along with the Clippers on top of Brooklyn's long-held interest ... but Hachimura had a clear intention to stay in LA after 3 1/2 seasons as a Laker.

item.title: I'm told Monte McNair - the longtime Houston Rockets …
item.description: Sam Amick: In other LAC news, I'm told Monte McNair - the longtime Houston Rockets executive and former Sacramento Kings GM - has agreed to return to the Clippers in an advisor role. McNair spent last season in this same role, but was a free agent this summer.

item.title: Nick Martinelli signed a two-year, two-way deal with …
item.description: Nick Martinelli signed a two-year, two-way deal with the LA Clippers.

item.title: "I think everyone on our staff is so blessed to have …
item.description: Law Murray: Jay Larranaga on Tyronn Lue: "I think everyone on our staff is so blessed to have Ty as our boss, right? So, that's the first thing. And that appreciation for kind of how he treats everybody on a day-to-day basis, how he treats our staff, how he treats the players... You know, someone said a long time ago, like, he treats them so well that they almost feel guilty not to give an honest effort. And that's kind of what I try and do is like I'm gonna try and treat them really well, I'm g

item.title: Luka Doncic wanted Rui Hachimura back with Lakers
item.description: Law Murray: I’m told that Rui Hachimura was on the Luka Doncic preferred return list. Lakers wanted more defenders. Clippers had the cap space Lakers didn't have. Rui Hachimura wanted to stay in Ohtani's town. Matchmaker made a match.

item.title: The two teams that we seem to hear the most chatter …
item.description: Matt George: The two teams that we seem to hear the most chatter about in terms of potential landing destinations for DeMar DeRozan, the Miami Heat, who of course just traded for Giannis, and the Cleveland Cavaliers, who are a team I long thought could use the services of DeMar, especially in that second unit off of the bench. Of course, we've always heard LA ties, right? He is from LA, from Compton, very much a Los Angeles guy. So, we've always talked about the possibility of DeMar going home.

item.title: Warriors expected to check in on DeMar DeRozan
item.description: Anthony Slater: You know I see the Miami Heat. I anticipate them being interested potentially in DeMar DeRozan. The Warriors have already been told it's a guy they will look into at some point in the coming days, especially if they do not get LeBron James. There's some heartwarming potential landing spots right. You know the Clippers play in his hometown the Raptors. He's so famous. Those are two teams that compete that could use him. And the reality is he should be coming at such a bargain that

item.title: Dallas is sending Middleton to the Wizards via …
item.description: Shams Charania: Dallas is sending Middleton to the Wizards via sign-and-trade as part of a larger six-team deal that includes, in total, the Mavericks, Wizards, Clippers, Pistons, Bucks and Grizzlies. It involves previously agreed upon deals such as John Collins, Gary Harris and Taurean Prince (Detroit), Isaiah Stewart (Memphis), Santi Aldama (Mavericks), Caris LeVert (Bucks), etc.

item.title: "Mitch Kupchak, he believed in me from the first day. …
item.description: Kai Jones: "Mitch Kupchak, he believed in me from the first day. He told me that he really thought I was going to be a great player in the NBA. And even when I was waived and a free agent, I called him up and I said, Mitch, thank you for everything you did for me. And he told me, I still think you have a chance to come back into the NBA and be a great player. And I think that I did, showing my preseason with the Clippers and my time with the Clippers and the Mavs. I think that I showed that I ca

item.title: "I had some teams talking to me about possibly coming …
item.description: Kai Jones: "I had some teams talking to me about possibly coming in on an Exhibit 10 in the NBA, but I'm like, man, I would rather go play high-level ball overseas and make some good money than to do the non-guaranteed route which is what I had already did with the Clippers. It was a great experience, but you know, I felt like I had deserved more than that and I was like, I can go and play for money and still get recruited from there."

item.title: Peyton Watson on Clippers' radar
item.description: Another Western Conference executive said teams around the league are now waiting to see the Clippers’ next move. The Clippers have interest in Denver’s Peyton Watson, who is a restricted free agent. The Nuggets can match any offer sheet and want to keep Watson but could always opt for a sign-and-trade. The Clippers also want to keep their own restricted free agent Bennedict Mathurin, whom they acquired in the Zubac trade. -- Ohm Youngmisuk

item.title: According to what we've been able to gather from our …
item.description: There was no longer any doubt that Asvel was serious. The signing of Sylvain Francisco (who, however, has an active NBA opt-out) and the signing of Italian league MVP Armoni Brooks are just the first moves in a stellar transfer market aimed at giving Tony Parker the best possible roster to lead the Euroleague. A highly prestigious name, crucial to the French player market, has been added to this list. According to what we've been able to gather from our sources, ASVEL is ready to sign Nicolas Ba

item.title: Stein‬: I'm told that six-team trade involving the …
item.description: ‪Marc Stein‬: I'm told that six-team trade involving the Pistons, Clippers, Grizzlies, Bucks, Wizards and Mavericks is on course to be made official and announced shortly. More NBA from @jakelfischer.bsky.social and me: tinyurl.com/5b4w9jws

item.title: Per the NBA’s transaction wire, a lot of Pistons …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Per the NBA’s transaction wire, a lot of Pistons trades are now official. They’ve acquired John Collins, Taurean Prince and Gary Harris and traded Isaiah Stewart, Caris LeVert and Marcus Sasser.

item.title: Six-team trade involving Mavs, Pistons, Clippers, Grizzlies, Bucks and Wizards made official
item.description: The Dallas Mavericks announced today their participation in a six-team trade involving the Detroit Pistons, L.A. Clippers, Memphis Grizzlies, Milwaukee Bucks and Washington Wizards. Dallas acquired forward Santi Aldama and the rights to forward Tarik Biberović from Memphis along with guard Marcus Sasser from Detroit. Dallas traded guard AJ Johnson, a top-20 protected 2030 first-round pick (via Golden State) and a 2029 second-round pick (via Houston) to Memphis. The Mavericks also sent forward K

item.title: The @Detroit Pistons announced today that the team has …
item.description: The @Detroit Pistons announced today that the team has acquired forward John Collins from the Los Angeles Clippers and forward Taurean Prince and guard Gary Harris from the Milwaukee Bucks as part of a six-team trade.

item.title: NBA veteran Amir Coffey headed overseas, joins Hapoel Tel Aviv
item.description: Hapoel Tel Aviv announced a major signing from the NBA for the upcoming EuroLeague season. The club officially confirmed the arrival of former Los Angeles Clippers forward Amir Coffey on a one-year deal.

item.title: Kawhi Leonard trade will not be complete until the NBA's investigation into the Clippers is over
item.description: Shams Charania: Developing: The Los Angeles Clippers-Toronto Raptors trade centered on Kawhi Leonard will not be complete until the NBA investigation into the Clippers is over. Statement from the Clippers to ESPN: For the past 10 months, our organization has fully cooperated with an NBA investigation, participating in dozens of interviews, providing tens of thousands of documents, and facilitating access to our staff. While the process has been challenging, we have remained committed to transpar

item.title: On June 30, we reached an agreement in principle to …
item.description: Shams Charania: Clippers statement: On June 30, we reached an agreement in principle to trade Kawhi Leonard to the Toronto Raptors. We have since been informed that the trade can only be finalized if the Raptors' ownership group assumes the risk of penalties related to Kawhi’s contract that could theoretically result from the ongoing investigation. The investigation is ongoing, and we expect the trade to be finalized following its conclusion.

item.title: We remain confident that, when the facts are evaluated …
item.description: Shams Charania: Clippers statement: At the heart of this investigation are Joe Sanberg and Aspiration. We did not funnel money to Kawhi Leonard through Aspiration. Like many sophisticated investors, financial institutions, and business partners, we were victims of a fraud initiated by Sanberg, who has been convicted and sentenced to 14 years in prison. We recognize the uncertainty this has created and the impact it has had on our team, our fans, the Raptors organization, their fans, and the play

item.title: We would assume the risk of any potential outcome of …
item.description: Marc Stein: Raptors statement: Regarding our planned trade with the LA Clippers for Kawhi Leonard, the NBA league office informed us that as a result of the ongoing investigation involving the Clippers, we would assume the risk of any potential outcome of the investigation impacting Kawhi. In light of this, we will wait until the league's investigation is complete. The Raptors remain eager to bring Kawhi back to Toronto and look forward to a swift resolution for our players, our organization and

item.title: Raptors hopeful Kawhi Leonard investigation is resolved by Tuesday?
item.description: Michael Grange: So, based on some preliminary convos: 1) NBA wants investigation complete before deal closes to avoid unintended consequences (ie small chance Kawhi contract gets voided) 2) Barring THAT the deal will close as agreed. 3) Hope is issue will be resolved by BOG meeting Tuesday:

item.title: Shams: Expectation is Kawhi Leonard trade will be done by end of the day
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Shams on Kawhi trade: "The expectation around all sides is that this deal will end up happening at the end of the day"

item.title: “We don’t have a specific timeline for the conclusion …
item.description: Marc Stein: NBA spokesperson to @TheSteinLine on the league’s investigation into Kawhi Leonard’s contract with the Clippers: “We don’t have a specific timeline for the conclusion of the investigation but expect the firm to finalize its work in the coming weeks."

item.title: Hawks interested in Peyton Watson
item.description: Atlanta, sources say, has also shown some fresh interest in Watson, which would likewise require a sign-and-trade to make it happen. That said, whether it's the Hawks or the Clippers or any other suitor, sources maintain that the Nuggets are seeking compensation on par with what Utah received from the Lakers in their recent sign-and-trade swap that made Walker Kessler a Laker.

item.title: Sources with knowledge of the talks told me Thursday …
item.description: Sources with knowledge of the talks told me Thursday that the Nuggets' sign-and-trade asking price has to this juncture been too steep for the Clippers to deeply consider. As a result, sources say, there is an increasing possibility that the Clippers opt from here to focus on re-signing their own restricted free agent Bennedict Mathurin instead.

item.title: If Kawhi Leonard faces a real penalty here we could …
item.description: NBABase: Windhorst: “The Clippers still want to do this trade badly they're getting a whole bunch of draft capital in this deal the Raptors in their statement yesterday said they were eager they want this trade to happen but if Kawhi Leonard faces a real penalty here we could see the trade terms reopened and I think that's what the Raptors are trying to protect themselves.”

item.title: Sources say, under the impression from the league …
item.description: The Clippers explored Leonard's trade market for weeks, sources say, before coming to terms on a deal with Toronto. And ... They did so, sources say, under the impression from the league office that the eventual outcome of the NBA's ongoing investigation into alleged cap impropriety involving Leonard and third-party business sponsor Aspiration would not have any impact on trading their star forward away.

item.title: Baba Miller's deal with Clippers includes two guaranteed years
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Los Angeles Clippers signed Baba Miller to a 4-year, $9.61M deal, which includes two guaranteed years, agents Deirunas Visockas and Guillermo Bermejo of @gershbasketball told @hoopshype . This is the highest overall amount of any second-round pick in the 2026 NBA Draft so far.

item.title: Yanic Konan Niederhäuser (Lisfranc) had an offseason …
item.description: Law Murray: LA Clippers center Yanic Konan Niederhäuser (Lisfranc) had an offseason procedure as part of his recovery from March surgery to repair his right foot. He is in a boot still since they had to remove the hardware from initial procedure, but it is a normal part of the recovery process

item.title: Yet while the league’s handling of the situation isn’t …
item.description: The league ignored the public pressure that came from the trade being publicized, those sources say, and maintained its stance that trading for Leonard before the investigation was completed meant the Raptors were, as the team said in a statement Thursday, “assuming the risk of any potential outcome” of the investigation. Yet while the league’s handling of the situation isn’t seen as a sign that significant discipline is coming for the Clippers and/or Leonard, the potential for Leonard’s contrac

item.title: However small the risk might be that Leonard’s …
item.description: However small the risk might be that Leonard’s contract would be voided, that was reason enough for the Raptors to play it safe by cooperating with the league’s preference to close the investigation before finalizing the trade. The NBA has only voided the contract of one player, Joe Smith, and that was after the Minnesota Timberwolves signed him to three consecutive below-market deals with the promise to sign him to a longer, richer deal in the future. In that case, the league voided Smith’s con

item.title: Regardless, the sense is that both teams are not …
item.description: Regardless, the sense is that both teams are not looking to alter the terms of the deal. Leonard was already at the Raptors practice facility in Toronto earlier in the week, and was in attendance for Kyle Lowry’s retirement announcement on Tuesday. Ingram and Dick were in Las Vegas Thursday night watching the Clippers’ summer league team.

item.title: There is a general bewilderment around the league …
item.description: There is a general bewilderment around the league regarding how long this investigation has lasted, and it’s not unreasonable to think the players’ association would become involved if it continued long enough to jeopardize Leonard’s ability to plan his future — i.e., sign an extension, as his current contract is set to expire after this coming season. The league never suspended Leonard or placed him on leave during the investigation, so the possibility that the Clippers might look to trade him

item.title: The NBA informed the Raptors before they agreed to the …
item.description: The NBA informed the Raptors before they agreed to the trade that the investigation could cause issues with any deal involving Leonard, according to sources briefed on the matter who spoke on the condition of anonymity because the league’s probe is ongoing. Yet when the terms of the deal between the Clippers and Raptors were widely reported on June 30, it forced the league — and commissioner Adam Silver — to decide how to handle this unprecedented situation.

item.title: The expectation is that Brandon Ingram will remain …
item.description: AP Hoops: The expectation is that Brandon Ingram will remain with the LA Clippers once the Kawhi Leonard trade is finalized, per @JakeLFischer . Ingram being rerouted to a third team is not considered likely at this juncture.

item.title: LA Clippers have officially announced the re-signing …
item.description: Justin Russo: LA Clippers have officially announced the re-signing of Jordan Miller.

item.title: New NBPA executive director David Kelly says “it …
item.description: Jake Fischer: New NBPA executive director David Kelly says “it should not take this long,” in regards to the NBA’s investigation into Kawhi Leonard, Los Angeles and Aspiration.

item.title: David Kelly also said he does not think “there’s a …
item.description: Jake Fischer: David Kelly also said he does not think “there’s a there there,” with Kawhi Leonard, stating the union agrees with the Clippers that they expect the investigation won’t find any impropriety from Leonard’s endorsement deal with Aspiration functioning as salary cap circumvention.

item.title: "A lot of people are saying they didn't get enough, or …
item.description: "A lot of people are saying they didn't get enough, or whatever, but nowadays in this NBA, this is a business, they're trying to make their business become a better business, and without a lot of problems, and that's what they're doing," said Payton. "You're getting a lot of young kids now in these drafts that's coming up, so if you get four and five and six picks, you're gonna build around that, and that's what it is. You see how Oklahoma did it, they built around picks, you know what I'm sayin

item.title: Bogdan Bogdanovic: Two years. Countless memories, …
item.description: Bogdan Bogdanovic: Two years. Countless memories, lessons, friendships, and battles. Thank you everyone in the organization for believing in me and making me feel at home from day one. To every coach, teammate, member of the medical staff, equipment staff, and everyone working behind the scenes thank you for your commitment and for pushing me to be better every single day. And to the fans, thank you for your energy and support through every moment. Special thanks to @laclippersswell !!! I’ll alw

item.title: Chris Haynes on the Kawhi trade:: “The Raptors, they …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Chris Haynes on the Kawhi trade:: “The Raptors, they were very comfortable in making this deal but they did not know that the league was going to put a halt on the trade. Both sides still feel like this thing will be resolved, a trade will occur. Brandon Ingram, Gradey Dick, they're all on the Clippers bench as if they're official Clipper members"
